# Code of Conduct

This project adopts the **Contributor Covenant, version 2.1**, available at:

<https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/>

The Contributor Covenant is the standard open-source community code maintained by the Organization for Ethical Source. By contributing to this repository, you agree to abide by its terms.

## Reporting

Reports of unacceptable behavior should be sent to the repository maintainer via one of:

- **GitHub:** open a private vulnerability report via the [Security tab](https://github.com/jra2s394/cs-repo/security/advisories/new) — usable for non-vulnerability conduct reports as well, since it is the only private channel this repo exposes.
- **Email:** the maintainer's address listed on the repo profile.

All reports are handled confidentially. The maintainer will acknowledge within 3 business days and follow the enforcement guidelines in section 2.1 of the Contributor Covenant.

## Scope

This Code applies to all project spaces (PRs, issues, discussions, code comments) and to anyone formally representing the project in any venue.

## Why adopt-by-reference instead of vendoring the text

The canonical Covenant text is maintained by a third party and is updated periodically. Vendoring it means this repository would drift from the upstream as the Covenant evolves. The reference-style adoption keeps this project aligned with the current published version automatically.
